message:
chore: initialize beads issue tracking system

This commit adds the beads_rust (br) issue tracking infrastructure
for local-first, git-tracked issue management designed for AI coding
agents and multi-agent coordination.

.beads/.gitignore - Tracked vs Ephemeral:
Excluded (ephemeral/local-only):
- *.db, *.db-shm, *.db-wal: SQLite database files
- *.lock: Process lock files
- last-touched: Timestamp file
- *.tmp: Temporary files
- .br_history/: Local history backups
- .bv.lock: Beads viewer lock

Rationale:
- Database is regenerated from issues.jsonl
- Locks are process-specific
- Only source-of-truth JSONL is tracked

.beads/config.yaml - Project Configuration:
Configuration:
- project_name: opentui_rust
- default_priority: 2 (medium)
- default_type: task
- sync settings for JSONL export
- bv (beads viewer) display preferences

Purpose:
- Consistent defaults for new issues
- Team-wide configuration in git

.beads/metadata.json - Project Metadata:
Contents:
- project_id: Unique identifier
- created_at: Timestamp
- schema_version: For migrations
- last_sync: Most recent sync time

Purpose:
- Track project identity across machines
- Enable schema migrations
- Sync coordination

.beads/issues.jsonl - Issue Data:
Format: JSON Lines (one issue per line)

Issue Structure:
{
  "id": "ISSUE-001",
  "title": "Issue title",
  "type": "task|bug|feature|epic",
  "status": "open|in_progress|closed",
  "priority": 1-5,
  "created_at": "ISO-8601",
  "updated_at": "ISO-8601",
  "closed_at": "ISO-8601|null",
  "close_reason": "string|null",
  "blocks": ["ISSUE-002"],
  "blocked_by": ["ISSUE-003"],
  "tags": ["string"],
  "description": "Markdown body"
}

Benefits:
- Human-readable in git diffs
- Conflict-friendly (append-only semantics)
- No database dependency for viewing
- Agent-friendly structured data

Beads Workflow Integration:
1. View issues: bv (TUI) or br list
2. Find ready work: br ready
3. Claim issue: br update <id> --status=in_progress
4. Complete work: br close <id>
5. Sync to git: br sync --flush-only && git add .beads/

Why Beads?
- Local-first: Works offline, no server needed
- Git-native: Issues tracked alongside code
- Agent-friendly: CLI-first, structured data
- Dependency-aware: Block/blocked-by relationships
- Non-invasive: br never runs git commands itself

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+# Database
+*.db
+*.db-shm
+*.db-wal
+
+# Lock files
+*.lock
+
+# Temporary
+last-touched
+*.tmp
+
+# Local history backups
+.br_history/
+
+# bv (beads viewer) lock file
+.bv.lock

@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+# Beads Project Configuration
+# issue_prefix: bd
+# default_priority: 2
+# default_type: task
